notes:
vMLX 1.6.4

Prefix-cache correctness release.

Fixed:
- Prefix-cache hits no longer return an empty response. MLX binds every array to a completion event on the stream of the thread that created it, and the prefix-cache isolate-clone was being built on the API thread, so the model worker could not evaluate anything derived from it. The error was swallowed and the request returned an empty 200, which meant every cache hit on a single-sequence model (Laguna) generated zero tokens. The clone is now built on the worker that owns the stream.
- TurboQuant KV layers are no longer handed to decode by reference on a prefix-cache hit. TurboQuantKVCache is not a KVCache subclass, so the clone gate rejected it and returned the stored entry itself; because TurboQuant KV is monotonic-growth, decode then appended straight into the cached prefix and later hits replayed a polluted context (a repeated "capital of France?" prompt eventually answered "Berlin."). Truncation now rebuilds those layers as fresh independent caches.

Added:
- VMLX_SWA_TQ=1 (opt-in, default off): per-layer TurboQuant KV on the full-attention slots of mixed sliding/full attention models, leaving the sliding slots on their native RotatingKVCache so the sliding-window metadata survives. gemma-4-12B maps to 8 TurboQuant + 40 rotating, gemma-4-26B-A4B to 5 + 25, Step-3.7-Flash to 12 + 33. Verified byte-equal to the default path on all three.

Changed:
- ZAYA text models now reason by default. The architecture hint was hard-stamped off, so a request that did not explicitly pass enable_thinking rendered a closed empty reasoning block and the model never opened its reasoning rail. An explicit enable_thinking still wins in both directions.

Notes:
- TurboQuant KV does not compress during decode on any model today: the compression threshold defaults to 0 and nothing sets it, so a TurboQuant KV cache holds plain float KV. VMLX_SWA_TQ is therefore output-identical to the default path and stays opt-in until that threshold is wired end-to-end, which will change decode numerics for every TurboQuant family.
- Known, pre-existing, unchanged by this release: prefix-cache hits are not byte-faithful to a cold run on gemma (warm output is itself stable — this is the q4 stored-prefix round-trip), and gemma-4-26B-A4B's greedy answer differs solo vs co-batched at --max-num-seqs 2 intermittently.

Verification:
- Python suite A/B'd against the v1.6.3 base: exactly one new failure, the bundled-python drift check, which clears after re-bundling. Panel suite 2176 passing (3 pre-existing failures reproduce identically on v1.6.3).
- Live on Apple Silicon against external-drive JANG/JANGTQ/MXFP4 bundles: decode determinism, prefix-cache pollution guard, multiturn recall over cache hits, and image / image-stream / image-multiturn media all pass on gemma-4-12B, gemma-4-26B-A4B (MoE), and Step-3.7-Flash, byte-equal with and without VMLX_SWA_TQ.
- Both DMGs are Developer ID signed (team 55KGF2S5AY), Apple notarized, stapled, and Gatekeeper accepted.

DMG SHA256:
- vMLX-1.6.4-sequoia-arm64.dmg: 84eae02bf46e22111d4442f4241479be73647a64ae3e8c1f6d54d1c4cbb9b948
- vMLX-1.6.4-tahoe-arm64.dmg: 21a0ae1bf946ee8f60ffd216b16ec871ceb08da755ac90d04ab98d86d596deff

Flavors: sequoia (macosx_14 wheels; Sonoma 14.5+, Sequoia 15, Tahoe) and tahoe (native macosx_26 wheels; Tahoe only).
